q: Who was the first king of Portugal?
exp: He became king in 1143, after the Battle of Ourique.

q: In which year was the Carnation Revolution?
exp: On 25 April 1974 it ended nearly 48 years of dictatorship.

q: Who wrote the epic poem 'The Lusiads'?
exp: It tells of Vasco da Gama's voyage to India.

q: Which Portuguese navigator reached India by sea in 1498?
exp: He set off in 1497 and reached Calicut in 1498.

q: In which city was the Portuguese nationhood born (its first king)?
exp: Hence the saying 'Portugal was born here'.

q: Which is the longest river running through Portugal?
exp: The Tagus rises in Spain and meets the sea at Lisbon.

q: Which bridge over the Tagus opened in 1998 and was Europe's longest at the time?
exp: It is 17.2 km long, opened for Expo 98.

q: Which Portuguese city has one of Europe's oldest universities?
exp: The University of Coimbra was founded by King Dinis in 1290.

q: Which poet wrote under heteronyms like Alberto Caeiro and Álvaro de Campos?
exp: Pessoa created dozens of heteronyms, each with its own style.

q: Which earthquake destroyed much of Lisbon in 1755?
exp: The Marquis of Pombal rebuilt downtown Lisbon afterwards.

q: Which Portuguese region is famous for Port wine?
exp: The Douro is the world's oldest demarcated wine region (1756).

q: How many districts does mainland Portugal have?
exp: Plus the autonomous regions of the Azores and Madeira.
